senses.gloss: to set up, to lay, to erect, to raise, to build
to raise the vowel to /a/; to put into the accusative case
to raise the vowel to /a/; to put into the subjunctive mood
to proclaim, to declare, to arrogate to oneself (king, war, sovereignty …)
to concern, to be directed at, to focus on, to concentrate on
to swindle, to scam, to ambush
to strive, to labor, to toil

examples.text:
فَإِذَا فَرَغْتَ فَٱنْصَبْ
 وَإِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَٱرْغَب
examples.english: So when thou art free [from thy tasks], then strive [in worship]. And toward thy Lord and Master turn [thy] attention.

examples.text: عِنْدَمَا نَصَّبْتَ اَلْبَرْنَامَجَ، هَلْ أَنْشَأْتَ لَهُ كَلِمَةِ سِرّ؟
examples.english: When you installed the program did you set up a password?

senses.gloss: an array of stones or a structure set up as a sign or mark to show the way (especially in the desert)

examples.text: […]إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَٱلْمَيْسِرُ وَٱلْأَنْصَابُ وَٱلْأَزْلَامُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَٱجْتَنِبُوهُ[…]
examples.english: […] Wine/liquor and games of chance/gambling and [sacrificing upon or offering unto] stone altars/statues and [drawing or casting] divination arrows are surely an abomination, of Satan's handiwork, so shun it […]

examples.text: حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ وَالدَّمُ[…]وَمَا ذُبِحَ عَلَى النُّصُب[…]
examples.english: And prohibited unto you are carrion/dead meat and blood […] and what was slain upon the stone altars/statues […]
